channel.title: Posts on Gertm's blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Gertm's blog

item.title: Running Swi Prolog Http Server Behind Nginx
item.description: This post will show you how to run the SWI-Prolog http server behind an nginx reverse proxy. This assumes you have experience with nginx and already have a server up and running. SWI-Prolog has a built-in webserver in library(http/http_server). Of course exposing this kind of thing directly to the internet is never a good idea and most people put nginx in front of it.

item.title: Another blog reboot
item.description: Trying this again. Now it’ll be hosted on github and bitbucket pages, which means it’ll survive my umpteenth VPS reinstall.

item.title: Why I stopped using Linux
item.description: TL;DR: Updating video drivers screwed my system once too many. Reinstalled to Windows and never looked back. Now the PC works for me, instead of me working to keep it running. As most people of my age, I started out on my parent’s computer. One of the first ones I had access to was a Philips MSX. Quickly moved on from that one to a 8088, which I used for a couple of years.
